hypothesis: L-601 singleton dominance (7x outlier, 58.7% score share) makes genesis seed set fragile — removal fragments seed network
method: Parse 1150 lessons, build citation DAG (2843 edges), score top-10 seeds. Measure network properties with and without L-601. Test alternative: domain-diversity constraint (max 2/domain)
expect: L-601 removal: internal edges drop >50%, cross-domain reach drops >30%. Seed set has single-point-of-failure.
actual: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. Network STRUCTURE is robust (edges -9.1%, connectivity unchanged 8/10). But REACH drops -38.5% (52→32) and SCORE drops -56.0%. L-601 is a pure sink (4 seeds cite it, it cites 0 seeds). Real problem: meta monoculture — 7/10 seeds from meta domain, only 2 unique domains.
diff: Expected structural fragmentation: FALSE (edges -9.1% not >50%). Expected reach drop >30%: CONFIRMED (-38.5%). Unexpected finding: domain concentration is the dominant failure mode, not L-601 dependence.
fix_applied: genesis_seeds.py --diverse flag: max_per_domain=2 constraint. Achieves 3x domain coverage (2→6 domains) with <10% citation loss and equal cross-domain reach (52→52).
next: Update genesis.sh v8→v9 to use --diverse by default. Measure whether diverse seeds improve L→L citation in child swarms vs monoculture seeds.
